arts.ttl: League Two boss explains 'problem' facing Bristol City attacker after Ashton Gate loan exit
arts.sum: Doncaster Rovers boss Grant McCann has admitted the good form of the forwards in his squad so far this season is proving to be a "problem" for Bristol City loanee Ephraim Yeboah. Although Rovers boss McCann has been impressed by Yeboah's technical ability, the form of the likes of Kyle Hurst and Luke Molyneux in particular has made it hard for him to hand the City loanee opportunities.

arts.ttl: No new injury concerns as Watford aim to extend home run
arts.sum: The Hornets are the only team in England’s top four divisions still unbeaten at home since Tom Cleverley took over as interim head coach in March.

arts.ttl: Scott Parker shares Bristol City penalty verdict and makes relieved admission after Burnley win
arts.sum: Even with several enticing balls played into the away side's penalty area, City didn't really give Trafford a save to make after Hirakawa's second-half effort. Burnley manager Scott Parker has admitted he was relieved to get out of Ashton Gate with all three points after the Clarets managed to get the better of Bristol City on Saturday.

arts.ttl: Bristol City huff and puff but struggle to find quality to open Burnley up in Ashton Gate defeat
arts.sum: Burnley are well organised and have quality players, but I think if our final ball had been better and if our first-half performance had matched the much-improved second-half display, we would have got something out of the game. Whilst we did improve in the second half, with Twine getting involved and as I say Pring doing well, our best opportunity came with a counterattack and a brilliant ball switched across by Max Bird to Hirakawa.

arts.ttl: Bristol City go toe-to-toe with the big boys again as familiar problems emerge in Burnley loss
arts.sum: After two weeks without Championship football, Bristol City supporters will have been left deflated having seen Liam Manning's side return to action with a 1-0 defeat to promotion-chasing Burnley, particularly after a convincing win over Norwich last time out. It's a bit of a cliche, but goals are what win teams football matches and so far this season, City are not scoring anywhere near enough, especially at Ashton Gate.

arts.ttl: ‘A tough one to take’ – Watford vs Bristol City prediction: The72
arts.sum: The72's writers offer their Watford vs Bristol City prediction ahead of the Championship clash on Tuesday. The post ‘A tough one to take’ – Watford vs Bristol City prediction: The72 appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Bayo deserved to be on the winning side says Watford boss
arts.sum: Tom Cleverley heaped praise on striker Vakoun Bayo for "an absolutely fantastic performance" at Plymouth.
